verses.text: David begs God to hear his restless, mourning complaint under the pressure of his enemies. His grief is loud and raw. God invites His children to pour out even their most agitated cries.
David begs God to hear his restless, mourning complaint under the pressure of his enemies. His grief is loud and raw. God invites His children to pour out even their most agitated cries.
David begs God to hear his restless, mourning complaint under the pressure of his enemies. His grief is loud and raw. God invites His children to pour out even their most agitated cries.

His heart is in anguish, terror upon him, till he longs for 'wings like a dove' to 'fly away, and be at rest.' He wishes to escape it all. The weary soul dreams of flight, yet rest is found in God, not in fleeing.

His heart is in anguish, terror upon him, till he longs for 'wings like a dove' to 'fly away, and be at rest.' He wishes to escape it all. The weary soul dreams of flight, yet rest is found in God, not in fleeing.
He sees violence, strife, and deceit filling the city day and night. Society itself seems corrupt. The godly grieve to watch wickedness rule the place they love.
He sees violence, strife, and deceit filling the city day and night. Society itself seems corrupt. The godly grieve to watch wickedness rule the place they love.

His sharpest wound is that the betrayer was no enemy but 'a man mine equal, my guide, and mine acquaintance' with whom he had worshipped. Treachery from a friend cuts deepest. So Christ too was betrayed by one who shared His table.

His sharpest wound is that the betrayer was no enemy but 'a man mine equal, my guide, and mine acquaintance' with whom he had worshipped. Treachery from a friend cuts deepest. So Christ too was betrayed by one who shared His table.

His sharpest wound is that the betrayer was no enemy but 'a man mine equal, my guide, and mine acquaintance' with whom he had worshipped. Treachery from a friend cuts deepest. So Christ too was betrayed by one who shared His table.
He leaves the false to judgment, resolving, 'As for me, I will call upon God.' He turns from vengeance to prayer. The believer commits his cause to God and keeps on praying.
He leaves the false to judgment, resolving, 'As for me, I will call upon God.' He turns from vengeance to prayer. The believer commits his cause to God and keeps on praying.
'Evening, and morning, and at noon, will I pray,' confident God hears his voice. Steady, frequent prayer sustains him. Set times of crying to God anchor the troubled heart.
'Evening, and morning, and at noon, will I pray,' confident God hears his voice. Steady, frequent prayer sustains him. Set times of crying to God anchor the troubled heart.
'Evening, and morning, and at noon, will I pray,' confident God hears his voice. Steady, frequent prayer sustains him. Set times of crying to God anchor the troubled heart.

His betrayer broke faith, his words 'smoother than butter' yet his heart at war, 'softer than oil, yet were they drawn swords.' Flattery hid hostility. Beware the smooth speech that conceals a hostile heart.

His betrayer broke faith, his words 'smoother than butter' yet his heart at war, 'softer than oil, yet were they drawn swords.' Flattery hid hostility. Beware the smooth speech that conceals a hostile heart.

'Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee,' he urges, sure God will bring the bloody down but preserve the trusting. The whole psalm rests here. Roll your care onto God, and He will hold you up.
